subTopics.name: Classification of Numbers
specificCompetences: Apply classification of numbers in real life context
Explore properties of different types of numbers: natural, whole, integers, prime, even, odd, rational, irrational, composite
Investigate factors and multiples of numbers
Find HCF and LCM using prime factorization and listing
Create number patterns, games and puzzles
learningActivities: Exploring properties of different types of numbers using materials, puzzles, number cards, games
Investigating factors and multiples of numbers
Investigating ways of finding HCF and LCM
Creating number patterns, games and puzzles

subTopics.name: Combined Operations on Real Numbers
specificCompetences: Apply combined operations on real numbers in real life situations
Solving real life situations using combined operations
learningActivities: Solving real life situations using combined operations on real numbers

subTopics.name: The Four Operations on Integers
specificCompetences: Use integers in real life
Apply four operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) on integers in real life situations
learningActivities: Exploring the application of the four operations on integers in real life situations
Examples: temperatures, bank account balances, share market, goal difference

subTopics.name: Approximation
specificCompetences: Use approximation to make informed decisions in real life
Express measures to a required number of significant figures
Explore ways of expressing large and small measures
Use scientific notation
learningActivities: Exploring approximation in real life situations
Investigating significance of figures in measures
Expressing measures in decimal to required significant figures
Exploring scientific notation using calculators, activities, Bingo, card games

subTopics.name: Estimations
specificCompetences: Use estimation to make informed decisions in real life
Estimate measures and compare with actual measurements
Interpret absolute, relative and percentage errors and tolerance in real life
learningActivities: Estimating measures and comparing with actual measurements
Interpreting absolute, relative, percentage errors and tolerance in real life contexts

subTopics.name: Operations on Sets
specificCompetences: Apply set operations in real life context
Create sets using information gathered by learners
Explore different forms of set representation: listing, number line, set builder notation, Venn diagram
Explore and interpret various single and combined set operations: intersection, union, complement, set difference
Represent combined operations on Venn diagrams (up to 3 sets)
learningActivities: Creating sets using numerical and descriptive information
Exploring different forms of set representation
Exploring set operations through games and real life activities
Representing combined operations on Venn diagrams using computers, paper, board, chalkboard (up to 3 sets)

subTopics.name: Algebraic Expressions
specificCompetences: Apply algebraic expressions in real life
Explore and formulate algebraic expressions from everyday life activities
Distinguish variables and coefficients in algebraic expressions
Group like and unlike terms
Simplify algebraic expressions (addition, subtraction, division, multiplication)
Evaluate algebraic expressions by substituting numerical values
learningActivities: Exploring and formulating algebraic expressions from everyday life
Using letters to represent numbers: managing budgets, planning production, tracking expenses
Distinguishing variables and coefficients
Simplifying and evaluating algebraic expressions

subTopics.name: Operations on Matrices
specificCompetences: Apply matrices to real life situations
Formulate matrices from real life situations (payroll, shop records, distribution of resources)
Identify the order of matrices
Transpose matrices
Explore various types of matrices: singular, equal, identity matrices
Carry out operations on matrices: addition, subtraction, multiplication by scalar, matrix x matrix (up to 2x2)
learningActivities: Formulating matrices from real life situations
Identifying order of matrices
Transposing matrices
Performing operations on matrices in real life situations

subTopics.name: Angles Associated with Straight Lines
specificCompetences: Use angles associated with straight lines in everyday life
Explore relationships between angles and straight lines
Distinguish angles: corresponding, vertically opposite, alternate, straight line angles
Use angles associated with straight lines in solving practical problems
learningActivities: Exploring relationships between angles and straight lines using geometrical instruments, geoboards, computer software
Distinguishing angles associated with parallel lines
Using angles in solving practical problems

subTopics.name: Angle of Elevation and Depression
specificCompetences: Use Angle of Elevation and Depression in everyday life
Explore angles of depression and elevation practically using real life situations
Distinguish angles of elevation and depression
Find the angle of depression and elevation
learningActivities: Exploring angles of depression and elevation practically
Distinguishing angles of elevation and depression in real life situations
Finding angles of depression and elevation

subTopics.name: Pythagoras Theorem
specificCompetences: Use the Pythagoras theorem to solve problems in different contexts
Explore Pythagoras theorem through practical activities (a ladder against a wall, creating geoboard with squares and triangles)
Investigate sides of the right-angled triangle: adjacent sides and hypotenuse
Derive the Pythagoras theorem: a² + b² = c²
Solve real life problems using Pythagoras Theorem
learningActivities: Exploring Pythagoras theorem through practical activities
Investigating sides of the right-angled triangle
Deriving a² + b² = c²
Solving real life problems using Pythagoras Theorem

subTopics.name: Statistical Presentations and Measures of Central Tendency
specificCompetences: Apply statistics in making informed decisions in real life
Collect data from the environment using various ways
Distinguish between qualitative and quantitative data
Distinguish between discrete and continuous data
Represent data in different ways: tally charts, Histogram, frequency polygon, frequency table
Interpret data represented in different forms
Apply measures of central tendency: mean, mode and median of grouped data
learningActivities: Collecting data from environment: height, shoe sizes, favourite colour
Distinguishing qualitative and quantitative data
Distinguishing discrete and continuous data
Representing and interpreting data in different forms
Applying mean, mode and median to real life: test scores, average income
